title: Kth Element of Two Sorted Arrays

content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two sorted arrays **AAA** and **BBB** of sizes **MMM** and **NNN**. Chef wants to know the **KKKth element** in the array obtained after merging **AAA** and **BBB** into a single sorted array.

Chef does **not** want to build the merged array explicitly. He only needs the value of the **KKKth element** in the merged sorted order.

Your task is to help Chef find this element.

Function Declaration

**Function Name:**



**findKthElementfindKthElementfindKthElement**



**Parameters:**



**aaa**
A sorted array of size **MMM** in non-decreasing order.
**bbb**
A sorted array of size **NNN** in non-decreasing order.
**kkk**
The 1-based position of the element to be found in the merged sorted order.



**Return Value:**



Returns a number representing the **KKKth element** in the merged sorted array.


Constraints
1≤M,N≤1041 \le M, N \le 10^41≤M,N≤104
0≤a[i],b[i]≤1090 \le a[i], b[i] \le 10^90≤a[i],b[i]≤109
1≤K≤M+N1 \le K \le M + N1≤K≤M+N
Both arrays are sorted in non-decreasing order.

Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers **MMM** and **NNN**.
The second line contains **MMM** space-separated integers representing array **AAA**.
The third line contains **NNN** space-separated integers representing array **BBB**.
The fourth line contains an integer **KKK**.



Output Format

Print a single integer — the **Kth element** of the merged sorted array.
constraints: Both arrays are sorted in non‑decreasing order
content.inputFormat:
First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of:
- A line with two integers M and N – the sizes of the two sorted arrays.
- A line with M space‑separated integers – the elements of array A in non‑decreasing order.
- A line with N space‑separated integers – the elements of array B in non‑decreasing order.
- A line with an integer K (1‑based) – the position of the element to retrieve from the merged sorted array of A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the K‑th element of the merged sorted array.

solution.approach: The problem is equivalent to finding the K‑th smallest element in the union of two sorted arrays. Using binary search on the number of elements taken from the first array we can decide how many must be taken from the second array (cut2 = K - cut1). We maintain the invariant that all elements left of the cuts are <= all elements right of the cuts. When the invariant holds, the answer is max(left1, left2). The search space is at most min(M, K) and each step reduces it by half, giving O(log min(M,N)) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log min(M, N)) per test case, O(log min(M, N)) total for all test cases combined.
